topic: GPT-5.5 Batch API and Flex Mode: 50% Cost Savings for High-Volume AI Coding Tasks

Priority processing at 2.5x standard rate

1M context window supported
Batch API saves 50% on both input and output tokens vs real-time API
GPT-5.4 batch pricing: $1.25/1M input, $7.50/1M output
Batch results guaranteed within 24 hours, typically 1-6 hours
Step-by-step guide for submitting batch jobs via JSONL files
Batch + prompt caching stacks for 75% total input cost reduction
Four pricing tiers: Batch, Flex, Standard, Priority
Flex is synchronous unlike Batch but at similar cost
Flex may return 429 Resource Unavailable when overloaded (no charge)
Batch is fully asynchronous with JSONL file submission
Priority tier at 2.5x standard for time-sensitive production workloads

58.6% on SWE-Bench Pro, 73.1% on OpenAI Expert-SWE evaluation

Best suited for complex coding, research, and business workflow tasks
More token-efficient - higher quality output with fewer retries

Claude Opus 4.7 leads SWE-bench: 64.3% on Pro, 87.6% on Verified

Batch processing ideal for code review pipelines, eval grading, backfills

stats.fact: GPT-5.5 Batch and Flex pricing: $2.50/1M input tokens, $15/1M output tokens (50% off standard $5/$30)
GPT-5.5 scores 82.7% on Terminal-Bench 2.0, making it OpenAIs strongest agentic coding model
Combining Batch API + prompt caching achieves 75% input cost reduction (cached tokens cost $0.625/1M vs standard $2.50/1M for GPT-5.4)
Shifting just 30% of API volume to batch processing reduces total costs by 15% for high-volume applications
Flex processing achieved 8.5% higher cache hit rate vs standard batch, translating to 23% reduction in input token cost
Most batch jobs complete in 1-6 hours; guaranteed within 24 hours with higher rate limits than standard API
Priority processing tier costs 2.5x standard rate for guaranteed low-latency responses
40-60% of typical AI coding queries can be handled by cheaper models, reducing effective cost by up to 20x
